$schema_note: E7 ledger head-commitment conformance vectors. SHARED CORPUS — vendored by the S7v Rust independent verifier (terrene-foundation/envoy-ledger-verifier) per specs/independent-verifier.md ~198-200 + specs/runtime-abstraction.md E7. This JSON is the SINGLE source of truth: the Python harness (tests/conformance/test_e5_e7.py) loads it AND S7v vendors the same bytes, so the two never diverge. Each vector replays its ordered `appends` (entry_type + content) into ONE EnvoyLedger, then snapshots head_commitment(). head_commitment() is BOTH byte-identical across kailash-py / kailash-rs-bindings (same injected ledger instance) AND monotonic non-decreasing in head_sequence across the append sequence. expected_head_sequence_progression[i] is the head_sequence after appends[0..=i] (1-indexed count of appends). The Decision on shared-corpus packaging: a separately-consumable JSON fixture (NOT a git-submodule pin, NOT an inline-in-test corpus) — rationale in the test-module docstring + PR body.
spec: specs/runtime-abstraction.md E7 (head-commitment monotonicity) + specs/ledger.md Head commitment + specs/independent-verifier.md ~198-200 (S7v reuse)

vectors.description: One append; head_sequence becomes 1. Minimal non-empty chain.
Three appends across distinct entry types; 1->2->3.
Three appends with IDENTICAL content; sequence still strictly increases (head_sequence is per-append, not content-dependent).
Append carrying unicode content (NFC pipeline must canonicalize identically across runtimes); 1->2.
Deeply nested content dict; head_sequence 1 (canonical-form independence from nesting).
Integer-microdollar financial content across two appends; 1->2.
Content authored with keys in non-sorted order across two appends; canonicalization sorts identically on both runtimes; 1->2.
Eight appends; head_sequence walks 1..8 monotonically (longest E7 chain).
Content carrying bool + explicit null; two appends 1->2.
Content carrying an array; single append; head_sequence 1.
